lead: We Made a Drone Fly — Now It's Your Turn
sub: A one-button app already flies the Mini 4 Pro from our own code. That's your launch pad — build your own idea on top of it.

title: Flying Safely — Before You Arm the Motors
desc: The Mini 4 Pro is under 250 g, not harmless — spinning propellers cut

items: Indoor flight sits outside EU 2019/947 — no category, no distance rules; the building owner's permission is what governs
Propeller guards mandatory, no exceptions — bare props at ~14,000 rpm cut skin and eyes
No GPS indoors: the drone drifts on optical flow alone — keep ≥ 2 m clearance to walls, windows, lamps
Nobody under or behind the drone during takeoff, landing or a virtual-stick test — that's exactly where a code bug puts it
Say "props spinning" out loud before arming; hand stays on the RC even during an autonomous mission
Anyone who wasn't briefed and didn't agree is an uninvolved person — never overflown (A1, Module 1)
Virtual stick is the dangerous mode — the aircraft obeys your loop, not the pilot; build in a timeout / deadman check
Know the abort path before takeoff: RC pause > left stick down > power cut
Props off + enableSimulator first — the flight controller flies the whole mission virtually while the aircraft sits on the desk; then indoors with guards, then outdoors
Misbehaves in the air? Land immediately — don't debug in flight

title: One Button. A Full Mission.
desc: labs/dji-hello-world — a real app that flies a real drone

items: A single screen with one green button: FLY MISSION
Press it → the Mini 4 Pro takes off, flies ~1.5 m forward, spins 180°, flies ~1.5 m back, and lands — entirely on its own
~400 lines of Kotlin on the DJI Mobile SDK v5, built in a couple of hours
Code & full write-up: labs/dji-hello-world
This is the proof of concept your whole project builds on — the old question "can we even control a DJI drone from code?" is already answered: yes
You inherit registration, connection, KeyManager reads, virtual-stick control and landing — the hard part is done

title: Everything That Fought Back
desc: First flight took a dozen rebuilds — every single layer resisted

rows: App vanished the instant it launched
NoClassDefFoundError — the SDK secretly needs AndroidX on the classpath
Plugged in the phone — nothing happened
We used the RC's charging port; the data port is next to the clamp
The RC wasn't linked to the drone — you pair it once via DJI Fly
The drone had gone to sleep — power-cycle, wait for FC connected: true
Magnetic interference — step away from metal, then it flew

items: Every one of these is written down so you skip the pain — the complete troubleshooting log lives in labs/dji-hello-world

title: Your Assignment: Propose & Build
desc: The connection is proven — your job is to build something of your own on top

items: Propose your own drone-software idea — small but real, a capability the base client does not have yet
Build a working increment on top of labs/dji-hello-world (or the official DJI sample)
Write it up: what you built, what worked, and where the SDK stopped you
Keep it achievable — a narrow feature done well beats a broad plan
Simulator first for anything that flies — then the SAFETY rules from the start of this module apply, every flight
Getting stuck is welcome — the error and your hypothesis become teaching material

title: Idea Menu: Directions You Could Take
desc: Pick one, combine a few, or bring your own

items: Live dashboard: subscribe via KeyManager to position, altitude, attitude, satellites, link status
Log a flight to a file and visualize the track afterwards
Pull the camera video stream into the app; grab frames for image processing
A simple autonomous pattern — waypoint square, orbit — in the DJI simulator

body.card: Integration — ties into the backend modules
items: Push live telemetry to a REST / streaming backend (Modules 7–9)
Feed a UTM-style monitor (Module 6) with a real aircraft's position

title: What the DJI SDK Actually Gives You
desc: The Mobile SDK v5 exposes the aircraft as a set of programmable interfaces — here is the surface you build on

items: Takeoff, landing, return-to-home as direct commands
Virtual Stick — synthesise stick input to fly the aircraft from code
Waypoint & hotpoint missions uploaded to and flown by the aircraft
Adjust settings — shutter, ISO, aperture, exposure
Capture photos and video; point the gimbal from code
Pull the live video feed into your app for image processing
Subscribe via KeyManager to GPS, altitude, attitude, velocity
Battery, satellites, link status in real time (up to ~10 Hz)
Read obstacle-avoidance state — distances and warnings from the vision system
Browse and download photos / video from the aircraft's storage
The old ActiveTrack / TapFly / Point-of-Interest modes are not exposed as SDK-controllable missions on consumer drones like the Mini 4 Pro
You get the building blocks (virtual stick, telemetry, video) — you build the intelligence yourself

title: Set Up the Toolchain
desc: Four downloads, one account — everything is free

rows: Create a DJI developer account and generate an App Key
Start from the course client (already flies) or the official sample

title: The Guiding Question: Where Does the SDK End?

items: Whatever you build, find out what does NOT work — e.g. direct access to the motors or the control loop
Naming that boundary cleanly is at least as valuable for the lecture as everything that works
Compare with Module 4: a consumer SDK enforcing safety boundaries is a trust-level decision — DJI keeps your code away from the SIL-critical parts
Getting stuck is expected and welcome
Note the error message and your hypothesis about the cause
We turn exactly those notes into good exercise examples
